articles.title: US F-15 airman rescued in Iran after special forces operation

children.content: US President Donald Trump confirms an American airman has been rescued in a daring night operation by US special forces.

image.altText: An F-15E fighter jet seen in the sky

children.content: Donald Trump amps up pressure on Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz, threatening in an expletive-laden social media post to target the country's power plants and bridges if the shipping lane is not opened. Follow live.

items.title: Trump threatens to bomb Iran power plants in expletive-laden post

updates.title: Planes used in US rescue mission suffered mechanical failures, Reuters reports
Social media images appear to show US aircraft wreckage
'Very little' off the table in Iran war, Trump says

items.title: Threats tell us more about Trump's frustration than anything else

children.content: It's day 37 of the war in the Middle East. The ABC's Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran is in Jerusalem, where Donald Trump's threats to obliterate Iran are starting to feel like business as usual.

image.altText: A man wearing a helmet and flak jacket with the word PRESS on it looks at the camera with building rubble behind him

items.title: One ship paid US$2m to pass Iran. Now we're looking at a new era

children.content: If Iran's idea of charging ships to pass through maritime choke points catches on, we are entering a new era of much more expensive, more dangerous global shipping.

image.altText: Birds flying in the foreground as a large gas tanker is docked at sea with a large city skyline in the background.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: US intelligence says Chinese satellite imagery of bases is helping Iran

children.content: A Chinese company's publication of AI-enhanced satellite imagery of US bases in the Middle East is helping Iranian forces identify targets, US intelligence believes.

image.altText: Aerial view of large building.

items.title: 'Told my wife and daughter I was going to die': Christian villager relives Israeli attack

children.content: Bashir was injured when Israel attacked his home in a Christian village in Lebanon's south, where many residents refuse to leave despite threats of destruction.

image.altText: A man with wounds on his face, wearing a hospital gown lying on a hospital bed.

items.title: UFOs over the oval: The sighting school kids were told not to talk about

children.content: Flying saucers, balloons or mass hysteria? It's 60 years today since Australia's biggest UFO sighting took place at a Melbourne school. Witnesses are still demanding answers about what happened that day.

gallery.altText: A woman with short grey hair wearing a red top focus of graphics design image with 'flyer saucer mystery' newspaper behind
Two women and two men aged in their 60s hold paper drawings of UFOs
A imagery with graphics effect showing a young girl in a pink sweater. Behind is a focus on an undentified craft over an oval

items.title: Price crunch has some servo owners asking: 'Is it really worth doing this?'

children.content: Workers are reporting an increase in abuse from customers as service station owners say they're not making money from fuel price rises.

image.altText: External shots of a 7/11 petrol station and fuel pump with an out of order sign.

items.title: Commuters are switching from cars to e-bikes to save money on fuel

children.content: As high fuel prices continue impacting commuters, Australians are looking to e-bikes as an alternative form of transport, with retailers reporting higher sales.

image.altText: Sam smiles in a bike shop.

items.title: The cleanest air on Earth holds a powerful lesson about our climate

children.content: Perched on Tasmania's wild north-west coast, Cape Grim has been quietly tracking the world's cleanest air for 50 years. Its data tell an interesting story about how our world has changed over half a century, including rare success stories.

gallery.altText: A rocky shore in the foreground, with water and a large green cliff and mass of land in the background.
Graeme Pearman wears a red jacket and red flares, as he sets up a tower at Cape Grim in 1976

thumbnail.altText: A rocky shore in the foreground, with water and a large green cliff and mass of land in the background.
Graeme Pearman wears a red jacket and red flares, as he sets up a tower at Cape Grim in 1976

items.title: Angry customers who lost $10m in Zone RV collapse urge ASIC to investigate

children.content: With retirement dreams dashed, customers launch a nationwide campaign for the corporate watchdog to investigate the caravan company's former director over insolvent trading claims.

image.altText: retirees holding placards

items.title: How one extra tree too close to your house could cost you thousands

children.content: Tree canopy coverage is being boosted in every capital city, but one micro-level analysis has found a link between property prices and the proximity of trees to houses.

image.altText: Dark SUV driving down city street with lots of trees in it

items.title: A great gift or unexpected curse? Reflections on life as a new retiree

children.content: While time is retirement's great gift, it can become a curse for anyone who has devoted decades to a career and then suddenly wakes up with relevance deprivation syndrome and no firm plan on what comes next, writes Matt Eaton.

gallery.altText: Three men smile for a selfie. Two hold guitars while another holds up drumsticks while sat at a kit.
A man stands and looks back at the camera with Whitby Abbey in the background, green grass and blue sky.

items.title: Fire-affected towns welcome Easter visitors

children.content: Businesses in fire-affected communities of Victoria welcome holiday-makers and daytrippers, as the recovery continues.

image.altText: A smiling man in a shirt and cap leans on a farm fence

items.title: Wildlife rescuers scramble as hundreds of baby bats lie dying in outback

children.content: Rescuers are scrambling to save as many flying fox pups as they can, as hundreds of the native bats lie dead or dying on Top End streets.

image.altText: A volunteer feeds a rescued baby flying fox, which is swaddled in a colourful blanket.

items.title: Moved into a dirty rental? Here's what you need to know

children.content: Having to clean a new rental property before settling in is a common experience for renters, but landlords have obligations when it comes to rental cleanliness upon moving in.

items.title: Quiz: How well do you know your history?

children.content: Where was the Venus de Milo found? And what common household item first went on sale in April 1827?

items.title: How an 'experiential' class is helping older women's brains and bodies

children.content: Judy Taylor discovered dance later in life, but it has now become a source of joy, energy, and purpose.

image.altText: Two women dancing together their heads and hands are together.

items.title: Scattered rain, strong wind along Qld coast as forecasters watch TC Maila

children.content: The majority of Queensland will enjoy mild weather this week, with scattered showers across the east coast, while further north, Cyclone Maila is set to intensify.

image.altText: Rainbow over waves with lifesaver holding board

items.title: Watchdog seeks upgrade to 'harmful' youth cells to improve access to justice

children.content: South Australia's Youth Court cells do not meet "acceptable minimum standards of safety, dignity and care", resulting in child detainees increasingly appearing in court via video link, the state's youth detention watchdog says.

image.altText: A room with row of small cells with persplex walls on one side, lockers and a TV screen on the other. Two toilets in the far end

items.title: Japan assures Australia will get 'normal supply' of fuel

children.content: Fuel will continue to flow from Japan into Australia under assurances given during recent talks between the two countries.

image.altText: A woman in a blue suit laughs as she speaks into a microphone with a Japanese flag behind her.

items.title: Fiji cruise ship runs aground after hitting reef near 'Cast Away island'

children.content: Thirty passengers have reportedly been rescued after a cruise ship ran aground on a reef near the island where the film Cast Away was shot.

image.altText: A three-level cruise ship.

items.title: The 'cheese grater' bowls that fed a generation of skaters

children.content: A set of skate bowls with origins as an outlet for teens in a tough Geelong suburb, once visited by Tony Hawk, may soon be preserved long-term through heritage protection.

items.title: Kings break NBL trophy after dramatic overtime win as 36ers blow late lead

image.altText: Sydney Kings player Torrey Craig holds two figurines of basketball players after they fell off the NBL championship trophy.

children.content: After trailing by six points late in regulation time, a dominant overtime display has earned the Sydney Kings a 113-101 victory in the fifth and deciding match of the NBL Finals.

items.title: Bulldogs down plucky Bombers but lose two more players to injury

children.content: The Western Bulldogs hold off an Essendon fight back to triumph by 34 points, but will sweat on the fitness of Artie Jones and Tim English, who both limped off at Docklands.

image.altText: An AFL player in red and blue is kneeled over in pain

items.title: Raiders' season lurches towards disaster as Knights run riot

children.content: The reigning minor premiers are in freefall after Canberra slumped to a fourth-straight loss at the hands of a resurgent Knights outfit.

image.altText: A group of rugby league players in orange celebrate while players in green watch on

items.title: Lomax's false start adds to anticipation ahead of pro rugby debut

children.content: Fans will have to wait for Zac Lomax's Western Force debut, providing further intrigue about how the ex-NRL star will cope when he takes his first steps in professional rugby.

items.title: Tim Tszyu dominates Denis Nurja to claim title in Wollongong

children.content: Tim Tszyu has won the WBO international middleweight title with a lopsided unanimous points win over Albanian Denis Nurja in Wollongong.

image.altText: A shirtless boxer with a shaved head has his hand held up in victory, title belt next to him

items.title: Resurgent Jason Day back at Masters after career of near misses

children.content: After five top-10 finishes at the Masters, including last year, Australian Jason Day returns to Augusta in hot form as he wrestles with his clubs.

image.altText: Golfer Jason Day leans against a tree during a Masters practice round at Augusta National.

items.title: 'Easter bilby' populations booming across sanctuaries

children.content: The Australian Wildlife Conservancy says the threatened species has rebounded at multiple sites across the country.

items.title: The Kremlin's internet purge is like 'the Soviet era on steroids'

children.content: The Kremlin is increasing its efforts to dictate the narrative in Russia by shutting off the internet, restricting foreign messenger apps and punishing citizens for speaking out.

image.altText: close up of russian president vladimir putin during a meeting in the kremlin

items.title: 7yo girl dies after ATV rollover in Adelaide's north-east

children.content: The Easter long weekend also saw two motorcyclists lose their lives in a separate crash in the Adelaide Hills.

items.title: Terminal diagnosis hasn't stopped Joan Humble's final exhibition

children.content: After a lifetime of capturing Tasmanian landscapes, Joan Humble is holding her final exhibition. The 88-year-old has a terminal cancer diagnosis, but she is determined to keep painting as long as she can hold a brush.
